title: DNS issue, /etc/resolv.conf is not updated with "search dns.podman" on Podman 3.2.3-0.11
body:
**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug

**Description**

/etc/resolv.conf is not updated with "search dns.podman" on a setup that has podman and podman plugins and because of this all aliases are not resolved

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. Install podman and podman plugins
[icucos@localhost temp]$ rpm -qa | grep podman
podman-remote-3.2.3-0.11.module_el8.4.0+942+d25aada8.x86_64
podman-3.2.3-0.11.module_el8.4.0+942+d25aada8.x86_64
podman-catatonit-3.2.3-0.11.module_el8.4.0+942+d25aada8.x86_64
podman-plugins-3.2.3-0.11.module_el8.4.0+942+d25aada8.x86_64

2. create few instances that are using aliases and try to call them from the instances

`#!/bin/bash

podman network create backend2

podman create --privileged --name=instance1 --network backend2 --entrypoint=sh --network-alias service1 --network-alias service2 centos:5.11 -c 'tail -f /dev/null'
podman create --privileged --name=instance2  --entrypoint=sh --network container:instance1 centos:5.11 -c 'tail -f /dev/null'

podman create --privileged --name=instance3 --entrypoint=sh --network backend2 --network-alias service3 --network-alias service4 centos:5.11 -c 'tail -f /dev/null'
podman create --privileged --name=instance4 --entrypoint=sh --network container:instance3 centos:5.11 -c 'tail -f /dev/null'

podman start instance1
podman start instance2
podman start instance3
podman start instance4
`
3. From instance1 if i ping a service4 or service1 it fail to resolve it

**Describe the results you received:**
failed to resolve the host

**Describe the results you expected:**
to be able to ping by hostname

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
[icucos@localhost temp]$ podman version
Version:      3.2.3
API Version:  3.2.3
Go Version:   go1.15.13
Built:        Sat Aug  7 13:21:48 2021
OS/Arch:      linux/amd64
```

```
podman-3.2.3-0.11.module_el8.4.0+942+d25aada8.x86_64
```

**Have you tested with the latest version of Podman and have you checked the Podman Troubleshooting Guide? (https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/master/troubleshooting.md)**


Yes, i'm on latest version available in centos repo

**Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):**
physical

Do you have any dev repository that I can use to install the latest (3.4) podman on a centos 8.4? previously I tried with kubic but its not working anymore

comments.body:
After some search, I can see that is similar to the behaviour from this opened bug by @k1rb @Luap99 : https://github.com/containers/podman/issues/11962
He is using version 3.0.2-dev of Podman
This is a packaging problem. podman-plugins has to install dnsname version 1.2 or newer
@Luap99 as a solution should a wait for CentOS repo to be updated? Should i build  / install it from sources? If its a packaging problem will you forward it further?
I am not involved in RHEL packaging but you could try the packages from CentOS Stream. They should contain newer versions.
